paragraph: Wyckoff is solidly a single-family market. Fifty-one homes sold over six months at a median of $1,315,000, and this is a real seller's market. Nearly 59 percent of homes sold above asking, the sale-to-list ratio sits at 1.0375, and the median days on market is fourteen. Sellers are dictating terms, and homes priced right are moving fast. You'll see sales cycle through in under three weeks on average, with the quickest deals closing in six days. There's enough range in the market to matter. The low end dips below $600,000, while the high end reaches $3.3 million, so you're not locked into a narrow price band. A small condo and townhouse segment exists with five sales at a median of $952,800, but the volume is thin enough that single-family is really the story here. If you're house hunting in Wyckoff right now, understand that competition is real. The homes that hit the market properly priced don't stay available long, and buyers without a firm offer ready when they find the right property are going to lose out to someone who does.
